question: She ___ to school every day.
explanation: Third person singular in present simple adds -s: 'She goes'.

question: I ___ a doctor when I grow up.
They ___ to the park yesterday.
explanation: Past tense of go is went.

question: I ___ dinner when you called me.
She ___ her homework before dinner.
explanation: Past perfect before another past action.

question: If I ___ rich, I would travel the world.
She ___ when I entered the room.
By the time we arrived, he ___ left.
Had I known the truth, I ___ helped you.
Hardly ___ I reached home when it started raining.
Not only ___ he win the match but also broke records.
If she ___ earlier, she would have caught the train.
It is important that he ___ on time.
Rarely ___ he seen such a beautiful view.
No sooner ___ the bell rang than students left.
She behaves as if she ___ the owner.
On no account ___ you open this door.
He acted as though he ___ everything.
It is high time you ___ your habits.
Had she studied harder, she ___ passed.
He suggested that she ___ take rest.
So tired ___ he that he slept instantly.
Little ___ they know what was coming.
Were he ___ harder, he would succeed.
I ___ breakfast before I left.
explanation: Past perfect for action completed before another past action.

question: ___ apple a day keeps the doctor away.
They ___ in Chennai since 2020.
explanation: Present perfect continuous for duration from past to now.

question: By next year, she ___ her degree.
explanation: Future perfect for action completed before future time.

question: If it rains, we ___ home.
explanation: First conditional: present + will + base verb.

question: The letter ___ by her yesterday.
explanation: Past simple passive: was + past participle.

question: There is ___ milk in the fridge.
She can't decide ___ she wants.
explanation: Noun clause with 'what' for unknown thing.

question: If I ___ earlier, I would help.
The cake ___ by my mom.
explanation: Present simple passive for general truth.

question: She told me not ___ it.
explanation: Reported imperative: tell + not + to + infinitive.

question: He is the ___ boy in class.
explanation: Superlative for most extreme of group.

question: By the time we arrived, they ___ .
explanation: Past perfect for earlier past action.

question: As soon as he ___, call me.
explanation: Time clauses use present simple, not future.

question: In spite ___ the rain, we went.
Not only ___ she win, but also set a record.
It is high time we ___ .
explanation: High time + past subjunctive for urgency.

question: Were it not for you, we ___ .
Scarcely had we started ___ it rained.
The more you practice, ___ you improve.
He ___ go if he had time.
There ___ many books on the table.
If I were you, I ___ study more.
This is the ___ movie I've seen.
They have been waiting ___ two hours.
He will call you when he ___.
This book is ___ interesting than that.
She ___ already left when I arrived.
explanation: Past perfect for earlier past action.

question: I look forward to meet you.
She is used to wake early.
I am used to drink tea.
She has been here since five years.
If I was you, I would refuse.
Had I knew, I would act.
No sooner he came than left.
Scarcely I had begun when it stopped.
She acts as if she is rich.
Hardly we had left when it rained.
He is one of the student who works hard.
Not only she sings but dances.
If I would have tried, I succeed.
She would rather to stay home.
He is as stronger as me.
She speaks as if she know all.
If he was here, he help.
She insisted that he goes now.
If he would come, we start.
She talks like she is knowing.
She did not went to school yesterday.
explanation: After 'did not', base verb is used.

question: He has completed his work just now.
explanation: Present perfect does not combine with 'just now'.

question: I am knowing him for five years.
explanation: Stative verbs do not use continuous form.

question: She is more taller than her sister.
He suggested me to go home.
She has been working here since five years.
explanation: Since is used with point of time.

question: Each of the boys have done their work.
I am used to wake up early.
Had I knew the answer, I would have told you.
explanation: Past participle used in third conditional.

question: No sooner he arrived than it started raining.
She insisted to go there alone.
He is capable to solve this problem.
Rarely he has seen such a performance.
It is time he goes home.
She behaved as if she is rich.
The book was wrote by him.
If I was you, I would accept the offer.
explanation: Were is correct for unreal condition.

question: She is married with a doctor.
He is too much intelligent to fail.
explanation: Too much is incorrect with adjectives.

question: She denied to steal the money.
Hardly I had reached when he called.
He is addicted to play games.
She prevented me from to go there.
I look forward to meet you.
He is used to drive fast cars.
She has been working here from 2010.
explanation: Since is used for starting point.

question: She suggested me to go early.
She did not came to school.
explanation: After did not, base verb is used.

question: He was too weak for lift the box.
She is married with him for five years.
She did not knew the answer.
